req.title: Schedule performance review
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a strict temporal validation rule: scheduled_at must be >= now + 7 days. This yields three natural partitions: (1) dates clearly beyond 7 days (valid EP), (2) dates clearly in the past or too near (invalid EP/NEG), and (3) the exact 7-day boundary (BVA, split into exactly 7d which is valid and exactly 7d-1s which is invalid). A State Transition case covers the workflow: unauthenticated -> authenticated manager -> review scheduled -> notifications sent. A Decision Table case exercises the combination of role=manager vs non-manager crossing the date validity axis. Together these give positive, negative, and boundary coverage with determinism guaranteed by freezing time via a server-side fixture or a fixed UTC offset.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_beyond_7_days
preconditions: System clock is frozen to a known UTC instant via <freeze_time_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = frozen_now + 14 days (well inside valid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Response body scheduled_at matches submitted value
Notification records created for manager and reviewee
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timezone
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_beyond_7_days(
    manager_client,        # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for role:manager
    reviewee_user,         # <fixture> returns {"id": ..., "email": ...}
    freeze_time,           # <fixture> freezes server + client clock, yields frozen_now (datetime)
    assert_notifications,  # <helper_fn> asserts notification rows exist for given review_id
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) + relativedelta(days=14)

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = manager_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert "review_id" in body
    assert body["scheduled_at"] == scheduled_at.isoformat()
    assert_notifications(body["review_id"], expected_recipients=2)

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
preconditions: System clock is frozen to a known UTC instant via <freeze_time_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = frozen_now - 1 day (clearly in the past, invalid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Response body contains error code referencing scheduled_at constraint
No review record created in database
trace: R-WEB-092: past dates shall be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timezone
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(
    manager_client,
    reviewee_user,
    freeze_time,
    assert_no_review_exists,   # <helper_fn> asserts no DB row for given payload
    assert_no_notifications,   # <helper_fn> asserts zero notification rows dispatched
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) - relativedelta(days=1)

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = manager_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body).lower()
    assert_no_review_exists(payload)
    assert_no_notifications()

import pytest
from datetime import timezone
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_accepted(
    manager_client,
    reviewee_user,
    freeze_time,
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) + relativedelta(days=7)

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = manager_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert "review_id" in body
    assert body["scheduled_at"] == scheduled_at.isoformat()

tests.name: test_schedule_review_one_second_under_7_days_rejected
preconditions: System clock is frozen to a known UTC instant via <freeze_time_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = frozen_now + 7 days - 1 second (just below valid boundary)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Error message references scheduled_at or minimum advance notice
trace: R-WEB-092: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
BVA: upper invalid boundary scheduled_at == now + 7d - 1s
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timezone, timedelta
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_one_second_under_7_days_rejected(
    manager_client,
    reviewee_user,
    freeze_time,
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = (
        frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        + relativedelta(days=7)
        - timedelta(seconds=1)
    )

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = manager_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_schedule_review_non_manager_role_rejected
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with employee credentials and valid scheduled_at
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timezone
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_non_manager_role_rejected(
    employee_client,       # <fixture> authenticated HTTP client for role:employee
    reviewee_user,
    freeze_time,
    assert_no_review_exists,
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) + relativedelta(days=14)

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = employee_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 403
    assert_no_review_exists(payload)

tests.name: test_schedule_review_workflow_state_transitions
actions: Submit valid schedule request with scheduled_at = now + 14 days (state: review_pending)
Assert review transitions to state: review_scheduled
Assert notifications transition to state: notifications_dispatched
expected: Notification status for each participant equals 'sent'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timezone
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_schedule_review_workflow_state_transitions(
    unauthenticated_client,  # <fixture> bare HTTP client with no session
    manager_credentials,     # <fixture> returns {"username": ..., "password": ...}
    reviewee_user,
    freeze_time,
    login,                   # <helper_fn>(client, credentials) -> authenticated client
    get_review_status,       # <helper_fn>(review_id) -> str
    get_notification_status, # <helper_fn>(review_id) -> list[str]
):
    frozen_now = freeze_time
    scheduled_at = frozen_now.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) + relativedelta(days=14)

    authed_client = login(unauthenticated_client, manager_credentials)

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": reviewee_user["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at.isoformat(),
    }
    response = authed_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    review_id = response.json()["review_id"]

    assert get_review_status(review_id) == "scheduled"
    statuses = get_notification_status(review_id)
    assert all(s == "sent" for s in statuses)
    assert len(statuses) == 2
